title: Water Heating Fault
shortDescription: The dishwasher heating element or its circuit has failed, preventing water from reaching the target temperature.
fullDescription: Error code Er1 on Beko dishwashers indicates the water temperature is not rising as expected during the wash cycle. The control board monitors the NTC temperature sensor and expects a measurable temperature increase within a set time after activating the heater. Causes include a failed heating element, faulty NTC sensor, blown thermal fuse, or control board relay failure.

causes.cause: Heating element has burned out or developed an open circuit
NTC temperature sensor is faulty
Thermal fuse has tripped
Control board heater relay has failed
Wiring harness to heater is damaged

symptoms: Dishes come out cold and greasy after washing
Er1 displayed on the control panel
Cycle takes much longer than normal
Water in the sump is cold when cycle finishes

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher at the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Turn it back on and run a test cycle. If Er1 returns, proceed with diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the heating element under the sump area. Disconnect the wiring and test resistance with a multimeter. A good element reads 15-30 ohms. Open circuit or very low readings indicate failure.

fixSteps.title: Check the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the NTC sensor near the sump or heater. Disconnect and measure resistance. At room temperature expect 40k-60k ohms. Test at different temperatures to confirm it changes correctly.

fixSteps.title: Check the thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Some Beko models have a thermal fuse on the heater circuit. Test for continuity. If open, replace the fuse and investigate why it tripped (often limescale buildup).

fixSteps.title: Replace the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the spray arm and sump cover. Disconnect the heater wiring. Unscrew the mounting hardware and remove the old element. Install the new element, ensuring the gasket seats properly to prevent leaks.

whenToCallPro: If the heater element and NTC test fine, the control board relay may be at fault, requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dishwasher Er1 error heating element fix

faq.q: Can I still use the dishwasher with Er1?
faq.a: The dishwasher may run but will wash with cold water, resulting in very poor cleaning, especially for greasy dishes. Repair is recommended.

faq.q: Does hard water cause Er1?
faq.a: Indirectly, yes. Hard water causes limescale buildup on the heater, leading to overheating and eventual element failure. Use dishwasher salt and regular descaling treatments.

faq.q: How long does a Beko dishwasher heating element last?
faq.a: Typically 5-10 years. In hard water areas without proper salt use, lifespan can be significantly shorter.

title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The dishwasher did not fill with sufficient water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error code Er2 indicates the dishwasher failed to detect an adequate water level within the expected time frame. The turbidity sensor or float switch did not register the required water level. This is commonly caused by a closed water tap, blocked inlet filter, faulty inlet valve, or defective water level sensor.

causes.cause: Water supply tap is turned off or partially closed
Inlet hose filter screen is clogged with debris
Water inlet valve solenoid has failed
Float switch or turbidity sensor is faulty

symptoms: Er2 error displayed after attempting to fill
No water sound when cycle starts
Very little water in the bottom of the tub
Cycle does not progress past the fill phase
Inlet valve clicks but no water flows

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the water supply tap is fully open. Run the kitchen tap to verify water pressure is adequate.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet filter
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply and disconnect the inlet hose. Remove the small mesh filter from inside the inlet valve connection. Clean under running water. Also clean the hose-end filter if present.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With power disconnected, locate the inlet valve. Test the solenoid coil with a multimeter for resistance (typically 500-2000 ohms). No reading means the coil is open and the valve needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Check the float switch
fixSteps.instruction: The float switch is in the base of the dishwasher tub. Ensure it moves freely up and down. Clean around it to remove any debris preventing movement.

whenToCallPro: If the filter is clean, supply is fine, and the valve tests good, a professional should diagnose the water level sensor system.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dishwasher Er2 no water fill error fix

faq.q: Why does Er2 appear after the dishwasher has been sitting unused?
faq.a: The inlet valve can seize if unused for extended periods. Also, debris can settle in the filter. Run the kitchen tap first and check the filter.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause Er2?
faq.a: Yes. Beko dishwashers require a minimum of 0.5 bar water pressure. If your area has low pressure, the fill may be too slow and timeout.

faq.q: Does the Aquastop hose cause Er2?
faq.a: An Aquastop hose that has been triggered (by a leak) will block water flow entirely. If the Aquastop indicator is red, the hose must be replaced.

title: Water Temperature Sensor (NTC) Error
shortDescription: The NTC temperature sensor is sending out-of-range readings to the control board.
fullDescription: Error code Er3 indicates the NTC thermistor monitoring water temperature is providing readings outside acceptable parameters. The control board cannot regulate water heating accurately and triggers this error as a safety precaution to prevent overheating or ineffective washing.

causes.cause: NTC sensor has failed (open or short circuit)
NTC wiring is damaged or disconnected
NTC connector is corroded
Control board NTC input circuit is faulty

symptoms: Water overheats or does not heat at all

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off at the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Restart and run a cycle. If Er3 returns, proceed.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the NTC sensor (near sump or heater area). Disconnect and measure resistance. At room temperature (25C), expect 40k-60k ohms. Open or zero reading means failure.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring and connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the NTC wiring from sensor to PCB. Look for damage, loose connections, or corrosion. Clean connectors with contact cleaner.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the faulty sensor and install the replacement. Ensure secure connection and proper routing of wires.

whenToCallPro: If the NTC sensor tests fine, the control board may have an input circuit fault.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dishwasher Er3 NTC temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: Can Er3 cause the dishwasher to overheat?
faq.a: Yes. If the NTC reads lower than actual temperature, the heater stays on too long. This can damage dishes and internal components.

faq.q: Is the NTC sensor expensive?
faq.a: No, it is typically under 25 pounds/euros and is one of the cheapest dishwasher parts to replace.

faq.q: How can I tell if it is the sensor or the board?
faq.a: Test the sensor resistance with a multimeter. If readings are within specification, the board input circuit is likely at fault.

title: Overfill / Flood Protection Triggered
shortDescription: The dishwasher has detected too much water, activating the anti-flood safety system.
fullDescription: Error code Er4 indicates the overfill or flood protection system has been triggered. A float switch in the base of the dishwasher has risen due to water accumulation, either from overfilling in the tub or from a leak. The machine will activate the drain pump and stop all water intake as a safety measure.

causes.cause: Internal leak from a hose or connection
Water inlet valve stuck open
Door seal allowing water to escape
Float switch stuck in raised position
Sump seal or pump seal leaking

symptoms: Machine will not start a new cycle

fixSteps.title: Turn off water and power
fixSteps.instruction: Close the water supply tap and disconnect the dishwasher from power. Place towels around the base to catch any water.

fixSteps.title: Tilt and drain the base
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully tilt the dishwasher forward to allow water in the base tray to drain into towels. Alternatively, use a sponge to absorb water from the base.

fixSteps.title: Identify the leak source
fixSteps.instruction: With the base accessible, run a short cycle and observe where water is leaking. Common sources: door seal, sump connections, spray arm seals, inlet hose connections.

fixSteps.title: Repair the leak
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten loose hose clamps, replace damaged hoses, or replace the door seal as needed. Reset the float switch if it is stuck up.

whenToCallPro: If the leak is from the sump seal or wash pump, professional repair is recommended due to the complexity of accessing these components.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dishwasher Er4 overfill flood protection error fix

faq.q: Is Er4 an emergency?
faq.a: It can be if water is actively leaking. Turn off the water supply immediately. The flood protection system will try to pump out water, but manual intervention may be needed.

faq.q: Can too much rinse aid cause Er4?
faq.a: Excessive rinse aid can cause foaming which the float switch may interpret as a water level issue. Reduce rinse aid dosage and clean the base tray.

faq.q: How do I reset the float switch?
faq.a: Remove water from the base tray. The float switch should drop back down on its own. If it is stuck, gently press it down with your finger.

title: Drain Pump Failure
shortDescription: The dishwasher cannot drain water from the tub within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error code Er5 indicates a drainage fault. The dishwasher has attempted to pump water from the tub but the water level has not dropped as expected. This could be caused by a blocked drain filter, kinked drain hose, faulty drain pump, or blocked drain connection at the sink/plumbing.

causes.cause: Drain filter is clogged with food debris
Drain hose is kinked or blocked
Drain pump impeller is jammed with glass or debris
Drain pump motor has failed
Kitchen drain connection (spigot) is blocked

symptoms: Standing water in the bottom of the dishwasher
Dishes not clean due to dirty water recirculation
Gurgling sounds from the drain area

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom rack. Unscrew and remove the cylindrical drain filter and the fine mesh screen underneath. Clean thoroughly under running water, removing all food debris, grease, and buildup.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher out enough to access the drain hose at the rear. Check for kinks or crushing. Disconnect from the sink drain spigot and flush with water to clear any blockages.

fixSteps.title: Clear the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: After removing the filter, look into the pump well. Remove any glass shards, broken crockery, or other debris that may be jamming the impeller. The impeller should spin freely.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump is clear but still not working, disconnect power, access the pump from underneath, and test the motor windings with a multimeter. Expect 100-300 ohms.

whenToCallPro: If the drain filter and hose are clear but the pump is not working, professional pump replacement may be needed.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dishwasher Er5 drain pump blocked error fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the dishwasher drain filter?
faq.a: Beko recommends cleaning the filter after every wash or at minimum once a week. This is the most common cause of Er5 and is easily preventable.

faq.q: Can I use drain cleaner to fix Er5?
faq.a: Do not pour chemical drain cleaner into the dishwasher. If the kitchen drain spigot is blocked, disconnect the hose and treat the spigot separately.

faq.q: Why does my dishwasher smell bad with Er5?
faq.a: Standing dirty water in the tub breeds bacteria quickly. Clean the filter, drain the water manually, and run a hot cycle with dishwasher cleaner once repaired.

title: Turbidity Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The water turbidity (clarity) sensor is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error code Er6 indicates the turbidity sensor, which measures water clarity to determine how dirty the wash water is, has failed or is giving invalid readings. This sensor is used by the dishwasher's auto-sensing programs to optimize wash time and water usage. Without it, the machine cannot run auto-sense cycles.

causes.cause: Turbidity sensor is coated with limescale or grease
Turbidity sensor has failed electrically
Sensor wiring is damaged
Control board sensor input circuit is faulty

symptoms: Auto-sense cycles do not work properly
Error only appears on auto programs

fixSteps.title: Clean the turbidity sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the turbidity sensor in the sump area of the tub. It is a small optical sensor. Clean its lens with a soft cloth and white vinegar to remove any limescale or grease coating.

fixSteps.title: Run a cleaning cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Place a dishwasher cleaning tablet or cup of white vinegar in the bottom of the empty dishwasher and run the hottest cycle. This can clean the sensor and internal surfaces.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the sensor from underneath. Disconnect and test with a multimeter. The sensor should show a resistance change when the lens is covered vs uncovered.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve the issue, replace the turbidity sensor. Unscrew from its mounting, disconnect the wiring, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning and replacement do not resolve the issue, the control board may have a sensor circuit fault.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dishwasher Er6 turbidity sensor error fix

faq.q: Can I use the dishwasher without the turbidity sensor?
faq.a: You can run manual program selections that do not rely on auto-sensing. Only auto/eco programs that detect soil level will be affected.

faq.q: What causes the turbidity sensor to fail?
faq.a: Grease and limescale buildup on the optical lens is the most common cause. Running regular cleaning cycles and using dishwasher salt prevents this.

faq.q: Is the turbidity sensor the same as the water level sensor?
faq.a: No. The turbidity sensor measures water clarity (how dirty it is). The water level sensor (float or pressure) measures how much water is in the tub.

title: Wash Motor / Circulation Pump Fault
shortDescription: The wash motor (circulation pump) has failed or is not running at the expected speed.
fullDescription: Error code Er7 indicates the main wash motor (circulation pump) is not operating correctly. This pump drives water through the spray arms during the wash and rinse cycles. Without it, water cannot be circulated and dishes will not be cleaned. The fault may be in the pump motor, its wiring, the motor capacitor, or the control board drive circuit.

causes.cause: Wash pump motor capacitor has failed
Wash pump impeller is jammed with debris
Wash pump motor windings have failed
Control board wash pump circuit has failed

symptoms: No water spraying sound during the wash cycle
Machine fills with water but nothing happens
Humming sound but no water circulation

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filter assembly and check the pump intake for any debris that might be blocking the impeller. Broken glass and food particles are common culprits.

fixSteps.title: Test the pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and access the circulation pump from underneath. Disconnect the wiring and test the motor windings with a multimeter. Expect 5-30 ohms between motor terminals.

fixSteps.title: Check the motor capacitor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor hums but does not spin, the start capacitor may be faulty. Locate the capacitor (cylindrical component near the motor) and test with a capacitance meter.

fixSteps.title: Replace the wash pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is faulty, the entire circulation pump assembly typically needs replacement. Disconnect hoses, electrical connections, and mounting hardware. Install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the circulation pump needs replacing, the repair involves significant disassembly. Professional help is recommended if you are not experienced.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dishwasher Er7 wash motor circulation pump error fix

faq.q: What is the circulation pump?
faq.a: The circulation pump is the main motor that forces water through the spray arms during wash and rinse cycles. It is different from the drain pump which removes water at the end of cycles.

faq.q: Can a failed capacitor cause Er7?
faq.a: Yes. The capacitor provides the starting boost for the motor. Without it, the motor may hum but not spin, triggering Er7.

faq.q: Is it worth replacing the circulation pump on an older dishwasher?
faq.a: The pump typically costs 40-100 for the part. If the machine is less than 6-7 years old, repair is usually worthwhile. Older machines may be better replaced.

title: Spray Arm Rotation Sensor Error
shortDescription: The spray arm rotation sensor has detected that one or more spray arms are not rotating.
fullDescription: Error code Er8 appears on Beko dishwashers equipped with spray arm monitoring. The sensor detects whether the spray arms are rotating during the wash cycle. Blocked spray arm jets, a faulty spray arm bearing, or low water pressure can prevent proper rotation.

causes.cause: Spray arm jets are blocked with limescale or food debris
Spray arm bearing or bushing is worn
Items in the rack are blocking spray arm rotation
Rotation sensor has failed

symptoms: Dishes on one rack are not clean
Error appears only with certain rack configurations

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher and spin each spray arm by hand. Check that no dishes, utensils, or tall items are blocking their rotation path.

fixSteps.title: Clean the spray arms
fixSteps.instruction: Remove each spray arm (usually twist-off or clip release). Clean all jet holes with a toothpick or thin wire. Flush the arm under running water to remove internal debris.

fixSteps.title: Check spray arm bearings
fixSteps.instruction: With the spray arm removed, inspect the mounting hub and bearing surface. If the bearing is worn or cracked, the arm will wobble or not spin freely. Replace the arm or bearing.

fixSteps.title: Test the rotation sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If arms spin freely but Er8 persists, the rotation sensor may be faulty. Locate it near the spray arm mount and test with a multimeter.

whenToCallPro: Er8 is usually resolved by cleaning or replacing spray arms. Professional help is rarely needed.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dishwasher Er8 spray arm not spinning error fix

faq.q: How do I clean spray arm holes?
faq.a: Remove the spray arm and use a toothpick or thin wire to clear each jet hole. Then flush under a tap. Do not use sharp metal tools that could enlarge the holes.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher without the upper spray arm?
faq.a: Technically yes, but the upper rack dishes will not be cleaned. It is not recommended as it wastes water and energy.

faq.q: How often should I clean the spray arms?
faq.a: Clean them every 1-2 months, or whenever you notice dishes on certain racks are not being cleaned properly.

title: Control Board Communication Error
shortDescription: The main control board has detected a communication fault between its modules.
fullDescription: Error code Er9 indicates an internal communication error within the control system. On models with separate display and main boards, this means the serial link between them has failed. On single-board models, it indicates an internal processor or bus fault.

causes.cause: Ribbon cable between display and main board is loose
Display board has failed
Main control board communication port is faulty
Electromagnetic interference disrupting signals

symptoms: Display flickering or showing garbled text
Machine will not start any cycle

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power for 10 minutes. Reconnect and test. Communication glitches often clear with a full reset.

fixSteps.title: Reseat connections
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door panel to access the control boards. Disconnect and firmly reseat the ribbon cable or connector between the display module and main PCB.

fixSteps.title: Check for interference
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the dishwasher is not on a shared circuit with high-interference devices. Move the machine to a dedicated power outlet if possible.

fixSteps.title: Replace the display module
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating does not help, the display module may need replacement. Remove the door panel screws, disconnect the module, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If a power reset and cable reseat do not fix Er9, professional board diagnosis is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dishwasher Er9 control board communication error fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause Er9?
faq.a: Yes, power surges can damage communication circuits. A surge protector is recommended for all modern dishwashers.

faq.q: Is Er9 the same as a dead display?
faq.a: Not exactly. Er9 specifically means the boards can partially communicate but data is corrupted. A completely dead display may indicate a different power supply issue.

faq.q: How much does a Beko dishwasher control board cost?
faq.a: Main control boards typically cost 70-180 pounds/euros. Display modules are cheaper at 30-80 pounds/euros.

title: Heating System Error
shortDescription: The heating element circuit has detected a fault during the wash or rinse cycle.
fullDescription: Error code E01 on newer Beko dishwasher models uses the same fault detection as Er1 on older models. The control board has detected that water temperature is not rising as expected. This applies to models using the E-prefix error code format. The causes, diagnosis, and repair are identical to Er1.

causes.cause: Heating element open circuit
NTC sensor failure
Thermal fuse blown
PCB heater relay failure

symptoms: Cold water at end of cycle

fixSteps.title: Reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Power off at the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then restart.

fixSteps.title: Test heating element resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Access the heater under the sump. Test with multimeter: expect 15-30 ohms. Also check earth leakage between terminals and element body.

fixSteps.title: Test NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure NTC resistance at room temperature: expect 40k-60k ohms.

fixSteps.title: Replace failed component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the heating element, NTC sensor, or thermal fuse depending on which tested faulty.

whenToCallPro: If heater and NTC test fine, the PCB relay needs professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dishwasher E01 heating element error fix

faq.q: Is E01 the same as Er1?
faq.a: Yes, they indicate the same fault. Beko transitioned from Er-prefix to E-prefix error codes on newer models. The underlying issue is identical.

faq.q: Will the dishwasher still clean with E01?
faq.a: It will run but use cold water, resulting in poor cleaning, especially for greasy items.

faq.q: How can I prevent heater failure?
faq.a: Use dishwasher salt to prevent limescale buildup, run monthly hot cleaning cycles, and avoid overloading which restricts water flow around the heater.

title: Water Inlet Timeout (New Format)
shortDescription: The dishwasher could not fill with water within the expected time on models using the E-prefix format.
fullDescription: Error code E03 on newer Beko dishwasher models indicates a water fill timeout. The control board waited for the water level sensor to confirm adequate filling but the threshold was not reached. This error corresponds to Er2 on older models but uses the updated E-prefix format.

causes.cause: Water tap closed or supply interrupted
Inlet valve filter clogged
Inlet valve solenoid failed
Aquastop hose activated

symptoms: Empty tub at end of cycle
Error appears within 2-3 minutes of starting

fixSteps.title: Verify water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Open the supply tap fully and check household water pressure by running the kitchen tap.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet filter
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water, disconnect inlet hose, remove mesh filter, clean and reinstall.

fixSteps.title: Check Aquastop hose
fixSteps.instruction: If your model has an Aquastop inlet hose, check the indicator window. Red means triggered and the hose needs replacing.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power, test solenoid resistance with multimeter (500-2000 ohms expected).

whenToCallPro: If the valve needs replacing and you are not comfortable with the disassembly.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dishwasher E03 water fill error fix

faq.q: Is E03 the same as Er2?
faq.a: Yes, they indicate the same water fill issue. Newer Beko models use E-prefix codes while older ones use Er-prefix.

faq.q: What minimum water pressure does a Beko dishwasher need?
faq.a: At least 0.5 bar (7 PSI). Below this, the fill will be too slow and may trigger a timeout error.

faq.q: Can I manually add water to bypass E03?
faq.a: No. The dishwasher must fill through the inlet valve to operate safely. Manually adding water bypasses the level control and could cause flooding.

title: Drain Fault (New Format)
shortDescription: The dishwasher failed to drain water within the expected time.
fullDescription: Error code E04 on newer Beko models corresponds to Er5 on older models. The drain pump could not remove water from the tub within the programmed time. Most commonly caused by a blocked filter or drain hose.

causes.cause: Drain filter blocked with food debris
Drain hose kinked or blocked
Drain pump impeller jammed
Drain pump motor failed

symptoms: Water standing in the bottom of the tub

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom rack, unscrew the filter assembly, and clean thoroughly under running water.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the hose for kinks. Disconnect from sink drain spigot and flush to clear blockages.

fixSteps.title: Clear pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With filter removed, check the pump well for glass shards, bones, or other debris jamming the impeller.

fixSteps.title: Test drain pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power, access pump from underneath, test motor windings with multimeter (100-300 ohms expected).

whenToCallPro: If filter and hose are clear but pump is not working.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dishwasher E04 drain error fix

faq.q: How do I manually drain the dishwasher?
faq.a: Scoop out water with a cup and sponge, then clean the filter. You can also remove the drain hose from the sink spigot and lower it into a bucket.

faq.q: Should I scrape dishes before loading?
faq.a: Yes. Remove large food debris before loading. You do not need to fully rinse, but scraping prevents filter blockages that cause drain errors.

faq.q: Can garbage disposal blockage cause E04?
faq.a: Yes. If the dishwasher drains through a garbage disposal connection, a blocked disposal will prevent drainage. Run the disposal to clear it.

title: Door Latch / Switch Fault
shortDescription: The door switch does not detect the door as properly closed and latched.
fullDescription: Error code E05 on newer Beko dishwashers indicates the door latch switch is not confirming proper closure. The dishwasher will not start a cycle if the door is not securely latched, as water would leak out. The door latch mechanism, switch, or wiring may be at fault.

causes.cause: Door latch is worn or broken
Door strike plate is misaligned
Door switch has failed electrically
Wiring to door switch is damaged

symptoms: Door feels loose or does not click shut
Machine does not start when button is pressed
Water leaks from the door during operation

fixSteps.title: Check door closure
fixSteps.instruction: Close the door firmly and ensure the latch clicks into place. Look for any obstructions or rack items preventing full closure.

fixSteps.title: Adjust the strike plate
fixSteps.instruction: If the latch does not engage the strike plate properly, loosen the strike mounting screws, adjust position, and retighten.

fixSteps.title: Test the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the inner door panel to access the door switch. Test with a multimeter for continuity when the latch is engaged.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door latch assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the latch mechanism or switch is faulty, replace the entire door latch assembly. Remove mounting screws, disconnect wiring, and install the new unit.

whenToCallPro: If latch replacement does not fix E05, the control board door switch input may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dishwasher E05 door latch error fix

faq.q: Can a warped door cause E05?
faq.a: Yes. If the door has been hit or the hinges are loose, the door may not close flush with the tub, preventing the latch from engaging. Check door alignment.

faq.q: Is E05 a safety feature?
faq.a: Yes. The door switch prevents the dishwasher from running with an open door, which would cause water to flood onto the floor.

faq.q: How do I know if it is the latch or the switch?
faq.a: If the door clicks firmly when closed, the latch is likely fine and the switch is suspect. If the door does not click, the latch mechanism is worn.

title: Rinse Aid Dispenser Motor Fault
shortDescription: The rinse aid dispenser motor or mechanism has failed.
fullDescription: Error code E10 indicates the motorized rinse aid dispenser is not functioning correctly. The control board has detected that the dispenser motor is not responding to commands or the mechanism is jammed. This affects drying performance but does not prevent washing.

causes.cause: Rinse aid dispenser motor has failed
Dispenser mechanism is jammed with crystallized rinse aid
Dispenser wiring is damaged
Control board dispenser circuit fault

symptoms: Dishes have water spots after drying
Rinse aid level does not decrease

fixSteps.title: Clean the dispenser
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rinse aid dispenser cap. Clean around the opening and inside the dispensing mechanism with warm water and a soft brush to dissolve any crystallized rinse aid.

fixSteps.title: Check dispenser operation
fixSteps.instruction: With the door open and the machine powered on (carefully), select a cycle. Listen for the dispenser motor clicking during the rinse phase. If no sound, the motor may be faulty.

fixSteps.title: Test the dispenser motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the dispenser motor behind the inner door panel. Disconnect and test with a multimeter for resistance and continuity.

fixSteps.title: Replace the dispenser assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor or mechanism is faulty, replace the entire dispenser unit. Remove the inner door panel screws, disconnect wiring, remove mounting screws, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the dispenser unit needs replacing and you are not comfortable removing the inner door panel.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dishwasher E10 rinse aid dispenser error fix

faq.q: Can I still wash dishes with E10?
faq.a: Yes. The wash function is not affected. However, dishes will not dry as well and may have water spots without rinse aid.

faq.q: Can I add rinse aid manually?
faq.a: You can place a small amount of rinse aid in the bottom of the tub before each cycle as a temporary workaround.

faq.q: What causes the dispenser to jam?
faq.a: Old or low-quality rinse aid can crystallize inside the dispenser mechanism, especially if the machine is used infrequently. Clean the dispenser regularly.
